task: Look for the most useful reviews of the highest-rated anti-reflective TVs with screen sizes from 55" to 64" and prices ranging from $300 to $1500.
thoughts: I can see the Samsung website has loaded. Let me first close the cookie banner, then search for anti-reflective TVs.
Cookie banner dismissed. Now let me search for anti-reflective TVs using the search bar.
Cookie banner dismissed. Now let me search for anti-reflective TVs using the search bar.
Cookie banner dismissed. Now let me search for anti-reflective TVs using the search bar.
It seems the search only captured "flective TV" instead of "anti-reflective TV". Let me search again with the correct term.
The search box is now open. Let me type the search query properly.
The search box is now open. Let me type the search query properly.
I see 535 results. The results are too broad. Let me navigate directly to Samsung's TV section to find anti-reflective TVs with proper filters. Let me click on "TV & AV" in the navigation.

I can see the All TVs page with 22 results and various filter options. Let me first filter by "Diagonal Screen Size" to select 55" to 64", then filter by price, and look for anti-reflective features. Let me start with the screen size filter.
I can see the screen size dropdown. I need to select "55" - 64"". Let me click on it.
I've selected the 55" - 64" filter, and it now shows 13 results. Now let me close this dropdown and apply the price filter. Let me click elsewhere to close the dropdown, then click on "Price".
Good, the dropdown is closed and I can see "55" - 64"" filter is applied with 13 results. Now let me apply the Price filter for $300-$1500. Let me click on the "Price" dropdown.

I can see the price filter options: Under $500, $500-$1000, $1000-$2000, $2000-$4000. Since I need $300-$1500, I should select "Under $500", "$500 - $1000", and "$1000 - $2000" (which will cover prices up to $2000, but I need up to $1500). Let me select the first three to get the broadest coverage and manually check prices later. Let me select "Under $500" first.
"Under $500" is selected and shows 4 results. I also need to add "$500 - $1000" and "$1000 - $2000" to cover the full $300-$1500 range. Let me select those too.
Now I have "Under $500" and "$500 - $1000" selected, showing 9 results. Let me also add "$1000 - $2000" to cover up to $1500.

Now I have all three price ranges selected (Under $500, $500-$1000, $1000-$2000) with 12 results. Now I need to also filter by "Key category features" to find anti-reflective TVs. Let me close this dropdown first and then check the features filter.
Now I have 4 filters applied and 12 results. Let me check the "Key category features" filter for anti-reflective options.
I can see the "Key category features" dropdown and there's an "Anti-Reflection" option! Let me select it.
It seems the checkbox wasn't clicked properly. Let me click directly on the "Anti-Reflection" checkbox.

The "Anti-Reflection" filter is now applied (checked in blue) and I can see 4 results with 5 filters active. The filters show: Under $500, $500-$1000, $1000-$2000, 55"-64", and Anti-Reflection. Now I need to sort by highest rating. Let me close this dropdown and sort by rating.
It looks like my click accidentally navigated to the OLED TVs page and removed my filters. I need to go back to the All TVs page and reapply my filters. Let me go back.

I'm back on the filtered page with all 5 filters applied: Under $500, $500-$1000, $1000-$2000, 55"-64", and Anti-Reflection. There are 4 results. Now I need to sort by highest rated. Let me click on "Sort Recommended" to change the sort order.
I can see the sort options: Newest, Recommended, Price: High to Low, Price: Low to High, and "Highest Rated". Let me select "Highest Rated".

Now I have the results sorted by "Highest Rated" with all 5 filters active (Under $500, $500-$1000, $1000-$2000, 55"-64", Anti-Reflection) showing 4 results. Let me scroll down to see the TV listings with their names, ratings, and prices.

Excellent! Now I can see all 4 anti-reflective TVs sorted by highest rated, with sizes 55"-64" and prices in the target range. Here are the 4 TVs:

1. **55 Inch Class OLED S85F** - 4K Samsung Vision AI Smart TV (2025) - ★4.8 (181 reviews) - $X99.99 (Save $500)
2. **55 Inch Class Neo QLED QN90F** - 4K Samsung Vision AI Smart TV (2025) - ★4.6 (406 reviews) - $1,299.99 (Save $500)
3. **55 Inch Class OLED S90F** - 4K Samsung Vision AI Smart TV (2025) - ★4.5 (337 reviews) - $1,099.99 (Save $700)
4. **55 Inch Class Neo QLED QN70F** - 4K Samsung Vision AI Smart TV (2025) - ★4.5 (205 reviews) - $699.99 (Save $200)

The highest rated is the OLED S85F with 4.8 stars. Let me click on it to see its reviews and find the most useful ones.

I'm on the 55 Inch Class OLED S85F product page. It shows $999.99 (was $1,499.99, Save $500.00). This is the highest-rated anti-reflective TV. Now I need to scroll down to find the reviews section and look for the most useful reviews.
I'm still in the product description area. I need to scroll further down to find the reviews section. Let me continue scrolling.
Still in the product details. I need to scroll much further to get to the reviews section. Let me keep scrolling.
Still scrolling through product details. The reviews section is typically at the bottom of the page. Let me scroll much further down.

I see "SEE ALL SUPPORT" and "Related Products" sections but no reviews section visible yet. The reviews may be loaded separately or may be higher up. Let me scroll up a bit to check if the reviews section was between where I scrolled from and here.

I see product features still. The reviews might be in a different section. Let me try clicking on the "Specs" tab at the top to see if there's a reviews section there, or look for ratings/reviews text. Let me scroll down more gradually from here.

I found the reviews section! I can see customer reviews. I need to look for reviews sorted by "Most Useful". Let me scroll up a bit to see the review summary/header area where there might be a sort option.

I found the Reviews section. I can see:
- **Overall Rating: 4.8** with 181 Reviews
- **Rating Snapshot**: 5 stars (153), 4 stars (23), 3 stars (2), 2 stars (2), 1 star (1)
- 72 out of 74 (97%) reviewers recommend this product

Now I need to scroll down to find the sort option for reviews (Most Useful/Most Helpful) and read the top reviews.

I can see the "Summary of Reviews" and "Average Customer Ratings" (Features: 4.8, Performance: 4.7, Design: 4.8, Value: 4.8), plus customer images and a "Filter Reviews" section with a search box. Let me scroll down to find the sort option for reviews (Most Useful).
I can see the reviews section with "1 – 8 of 181 Reviews" and a "Sort by Most Recent" dropdown. I need to change the sort to "Most Useful" or "Most Helpful". Let me click on the sort dropdown.
The dropdown didn't open. Let me try clicking more precisely on the dropdown arrow.

The sort dropdown is now open and I can see the options: Most Helpful, Highest to Lowest Rating, Lowest to Highest Rating, and Most Recent (currently selected). Let me select "Most Helpful" to find the most useful reviews.

"Most Helpful" is now selected (shown with a checkmark). I can see the first most helpful review is already showing: "Tv slow and freezes. Only 4 days old" by Domdom, 2 stars. Let me close this dropdown and scroll down to read the reviews in detail.
The reviews are now sorted by "Most Helpful". I can see the first review starting. Let me scroll down to read the full content of the most helpful reviews.

I can now read the first most helpful review. Let me capture this and continue scrolling to see more reviews.
